name: Austrian Kaiserschmarrn (Shredded Sweet Pancake)
steps: Whisk the flour, egg, milk, and sugar into a smooth batter.
Cook in a hot, buttered pan until set, then tear into pieces with two forks and caramelize briefly.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, egg, milk, and sugar into a smooth, thick batter.
Cook in the butter over medium heat until the underside is golden and the top is nearly set, 4-5 minutes.
Tear into rough pieces directly in the pan with two forks and cook 2-3 more minutes to caramelize the edges.
Top with the honey - kaiserschmarrn, torn and sugared rather than served whole, is one of Austria's most iconic dishes.

name: Austrian Bread and Cheese

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the bread and top with the cheese.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread generously, top with the cheese, and finish with a little black pepper.

name: Austrian Apricot Jam Toast

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

ingredients.note: cooked down into a quick jam

steps: Simmer the apricots with the honey and a splash of water until soft and jammy, about 10 minutes.
Toast the bread in the butter and spread with the apricot jam.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

ingredients_full.note: cooked down into a quick jam

steps_detailed: Simmer the apricots with the honey, cinnamon, and a splash of water over medium-low heat, 11-13 minutes, mashing as it softens, until soft and jammy.
Toast the bread in the butter and spread generously with the warm apricot jam - apricot preserves are a foundation of Austrian pastry, not just a topping.

name: Austrian Wiener Schnitzel

ingredients.ingredient: Veal, leg, top round, cap off, cutlet, boneless, raw

steps: Pound the veal cutlet thin.
Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, and fry in the oil, 2-3 minutes per side, until golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Veal, leg, top round, cap off, cutlet, boneless, raw

steps_detailed: Pound the veal cutlet thin and even, about a quarter-inch thick.
Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, pressing to coat well, and fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 2-3 minutes per side, until deeply golden.

Finish with the lime juice - a proper schnitzel is traditionally breaded in fine dry breadcrumbs, which aren't a distinct ingredient in this database, so a flour coating carries the crust here instead.

name: Austrian Potato Dumplings (Knodel)
steps: Mix the mashed potato with the flour and egg into a firm dough.
Shape into balls and simmer in water 10-12 minutes until they float.
Toss in the melted butter before serving.
steps_detailed: Mix the mashed potato with the flour, egg, and pepper into a firm, workable dough.
Shape into even balls and simmer in gently boiling water 12-14 minutes until they float and are cooked through.
Toss in the melted butter before serving - knodel are the standard accompaniment to nearly any Austrian main course with a sauce.

name: Austrian Goulash

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion, paprika, and caraway.
Add 1.5 cups water and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, paprika, and caraway over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add 1.75 cups water and simmer 32-38 minutes until fully tender.
Austria's own goulash tradition, absorbed from its long history alongside Hungary, tends to use a bit more onion and a touch less liquid than the Hungarian original.

name: Austrian Cheese Spaetzle-Style Dumplings
steps: Whisk the flour and egg with a little water into a thick, sticky batter.
Drop small spoonfuls into simmering water and cook 2-3 minutes until they float.
Toss with the butter and cheese.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, egg, and pepper with a little water into a thick, sticky batter.
Drop small spoonfuls into simmering water and cook 3-4 minutes until they float and are cooked through.
Toss with the butter and cheese, letting it melt through - kasspatzle, soft egg dumplings folded through melted cheese, is an Alpine Austrian classic.

name: Austrian Tafelspitz (Boiled Beef)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef with the onion and pepper in water until very tender, about 40-45 minutes.
Add the potato in the last 20 minutes and simmer until tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef with the onion, garlic, and pepper in enough water to cover, 42-48 minutes, until very tender, skimming occasionally.
Add the potato in the last 20-22 minutes and simmer until tender.
Tafelspitz, boiled beef served with its own rich broth, was reportedly a favorite of Emperor Franz Joseph and remains one of Vienna's defining dishes.

name: Austrian Veal Goulash

ingredients.ingredient: Veal, leg, top round, cap off, cutlet, boneless, raw

steps: Brown the veal with the onion and paprika.
Add a splash of water and simmer 15-18 minutes until tender.
Stir in the sour cream off the heat.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Veal, leg, top round, cap off, cutlet, boneless, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the veal with the onion, garlic, and paprika over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add three-quarters cup water and simmer 18-22 minutes until tender.
Remove from heat and stir in the sour cream for a lighter, more delicate goulash than the beef version.

name: Austrian Pork Roast with Sauerkraut
steps: Season the pork with the caraway and pepper and roast at 400F for 25-30 minutes until browned.
Warm the sauerkraut in the pan drippings and serve alongside.
steps_detailed: Season the pork generously with the caraway and pepper and roast at 400F (200C) for 28-32 minutes until well browned and the fat has rendered.
Soften the onion in a little of the rendered pork fat, add the sauerkraut, and warm through, 6-8 minutes, and serve alongside.

name: Austrian Cheese and Potato Bake
steps: Layer the potato with the milk in a small dish and bake at 375F, covered, for 30 minutes.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 10-15 more minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the potato with the milk, butter, and pepper in a small baking dish, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 32-35 minutes until tender.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 12-15 more minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Austrian Bread Dumpling Snack
steps: Soften the onion in the butter.
Mix the flour and egg with the onion into a firm dough, shape into a small dumpling, and simmer in water 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion in the butter, 5-6 minutes.
Mix the flour, egg, softened onion, and pepper into a firm dough, shape into a small dumpling, and simmer in gently boiling water 14-16 minutes until cooked through.
Semmelknodel are traditionally made from stale white rolls rather than flour - this keeps the same soft, savory dumpling in a form this database's ingredients can support.

name: Austrian Almond Cookies
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, mix in the flour and almonds, and shape into small crescents.
Bake at 350F for 12-15 minutes until lightly golden.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar until light, then mix in the flour, almonds, and cinnamon until it forms a soft dough.
Shape into small crescents and bake at 350F (175C) for 13-16 minutes until lightly golden.
Almond crescent cookies are a mainstay of Austrian holiday baking.

name: Austrian Cheese and Caraway Crackers
steps: Rub the butter into the flour, cheese, and caraway.
Add a little water to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into shapes, and bake at 375F for 12-15 minutes until crisp.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour, cheese, caraway, and pepper until crumbly.
Add a little cold water to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into small shapes, and bake at 375F (190C) for 13-16 minutes until crisp and golden.

name: Austrian Apricot and Almond Snack
steps: Toss the apricot halves with the almonds and honey.
steps_detailed: Toss the apricot halves with the almonds, honey, and cinnamon, letting them sit a few minutes so the flavors meld.

name: Austrian Apfelstrudel (Apple Strudel)

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Toss the apple with the sugar.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and water, roll thin, fill with the apple, roll up, and bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Toss the apple with the sugar and cinnamon.
Make a simple, thin pastry from the flour, half the butter, and a little water, roll it out as thin as possible, and fill with the apple mixture.
Roll up tightly, brush with the remaining melted butter, and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until deeply golden.
Apfelstrudel, its pastry traditionally stretched paper-thin by hand, is one of Austria's most recognized dishes worldwide.

name: Austrian Apricot Almond Cake
steps: Mix the almond paste with the egg until smooth, fold in the pureed apricot and flour.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps_detailed: Mix the almond paste with the egg and melted butter until smooth, then fold in the pureed apricot and flour.
Pour into a small lined pan and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Apricot preserves layered with almond flavor recall the filling in a Sachertorte, without the chocolate this database doesn't carry.

name: Austrian Rice Pudding (Milchreis)
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Milchreis, warm and gently spiced, is a familiar homestyle dessert across Austria.

name: Austrian Apricot Sauce (Marillensauce)
steps: Simmer the apricots with the honey and butter and a splash of water, 10-12 minutes, until it breaks down into a sauce.
steps_detailed: Simmer the apricots with the honey, butter, cinnamon, and a splash of water over medium-low heat, 12-14 minutes, mashing as it softens, until it breaks down into a thick, glossy sauce.
The classic accompaniment to Austrian dumplings and pastries alike.

name: Austrian Caraway Cream Sauce
steps: Warm the sour cream, caraway, and butter together gently until smooth.
steps_detailed: Warm the sour cream, caraway, butter, and pepper together gently over low heat, stirring, until smooth - do not let it boil.
Good over roasted pork or potato dumplings.
